informal_statement.text: Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

semantic_units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target.
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr.
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

semantic_units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat), binarySearch arr t = some i → i < arr.length ∧ arr.get? i = some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int), SortedLE arr → binarySearch arr t = none → ∀ i, i < arr.length → arr.get? i ≠ some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_sorted_precond (a : Arr) : Sorted a → True := by intro _; trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice is sorted in nondecreasing order (Sorted a).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_some_implies_index_valid_and_eq (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t := by
  intro i h
  -- proof obligation: connect the model result to index validity and equality
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some i, then i is in bounds and arr[i] equals the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_some_implies_eq_only (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → nthLe a i = t := by
  intro i h
  have : IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t := by
    -- from the stronger obligation, extract equality
    admit
  exact this.2
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some i, then arr[i] equals the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_none_implies_absent (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  binarySearch a t = none → ¬ (∃ i : Nat, IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t) := by
  intro h hn
  rcases hn with ⟨i, hi, hEq⟩
  -- proof obligation: show contradiction with the model behavior
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None, then there is no in-bounds index i with arr[i] = target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_termination (a : Arr) (t : Int) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_duplicates_allow_any_valid_index (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i j : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → binarySearch a t = some j →
      (IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t) ∧ (IndexValid j a ∧ nthLe a j = t) := by
  intro i j h1 h2
  -- proof obligation: both returned indices must be valid and equal to target
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If duplicates exist, returning any valid matching index is acceptable; both returned indices (when Some) must satisfy the success condition.
